Tests if dividing `false` by `null` results in `NaN` using an assertion.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test assertion that checks the result of dividing `false` by `null` in JavaScript. The expression `false / null` evaluates to `NaN` (Not-a-Number), and the `isNaN` function confirms this by returning `true`. The `assert` function then verifies that the result of `isNaN(false / null)` is indeed `true`. This code provides narrow functionality, serving as a basic test to ensure that the division of `false` by `null` behaves as expected in JavaScript.
